methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Dust is a Series B–stage AI & Software company founded by Gabriel Hubert and Stanislas Polu that has raised $70M+. Valuation benchmarking draws from a peer set of 5 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom) with valuations ranging from $500M to $32.0B, alongside 1 public comparable (PLTR).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression anchored to the $70M total raised proxy and applied a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, yielding $133M. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-stage AI & Software comparables produced a $5.0B implied valuation, with a reported range of $500M to $32.0B. The dispersion within the peer set reflects high heterogeneity in frontier AI funding; Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B and Physical Intelligence at $5.6B represent significantly later-stage or capital-intensive positions relative to earlier peers like Photoroom at $500M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $113M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.6B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around Dust's relative positioning within the AI frontier cohort—specifically, revenue traction, technical defensibility, and capital efficiency relative to peers. Stage-progression methodology provides a floor anchored to capital deployed; peer-median anchors to market pricing but remains volatile given the nascent stage and broad dispersion of the comparable set.
